typical_season_note: Prices lowest right after harvest (Oct-Nov), rise Feb-Apr
selling_advice: Hold stock past harvest glut if storage available; avoid selling within first 2 weeks of harvest season

typical_season_note: Fairly stable year-round; slight rise in dry season
selling_advice: Cassava spoils fast - sell within days of harvest unless processed to garri/flour
Processing into garri before selling captures more value and avoids spoilage risk

typical_season_note: Extremely volatile - crashes during glut (Dec-Feb), peaks in rainy season scarcity (Jun-Aug)
selling_advice: Highly perishable - avoid holding stock; sell immediately, spread sales across multiple buyers to reduce glut-price risk

typical_season_note: Similar volatility to tomato, peaks during scarcity months
selling_advice: Dry or process excess into pepper paste for storage instead of selling at glut prices
Prices lowest at harvest (Oct-Dec), highest before next planting (Jul-Sep)
column7: Milling before selling typically adds 20-30% value vs selling paddy

typical_season_note: New yam season (Aug-Sep) sees price drop from previous year's scarcity highs
selling_advice: Sort by size before selling - large tubers command premium at urban markets

typical_season_note: Stable, mild rise Mar-May before new harvest
selling_advice: Shelling before sale increases value but adds labor cost - weigh against local buyer preference

typical_season_note: Fairly stable, slight rise during dry season
selling_advice: Sell ripe plantains quickly - unripe green plantains transport and store better if market is farther away
